topic: Best Local LLM Models 2026: Benchmarks, Hardware, and Use Cases

key_points: Ranks Llama 4, Qwen 3.5, Gemma 4, DeepSeek V4, Mistral Medium 3.5 as top open-weight models
Covers hardware tiers: 8GB, 16GB, 24GB VRAM and Apple Silicon
Includes benchmarks for coding (HumanEval), reasoning (MMLU-Pro), and math (AIME)
Picks best models by hardware tier and specific use case
Includes Ollama setup guide for each model
Compares quantization options (Q4, Q5, Q8) and their effect on speed vs quality
Covers open-source LLM landscape: DeepSeek V4 vs Llama 4 vs Qwen 3.5 vs Gemma 4
MoE architecture dominates 2026: DeepSeek V4-Pro (1.6T/49B active), Llama 4 Maverick (400B/17B), Qwen 3.5 (397B/17B)
Qwen 3.5 27B: MMLU-Pro 86.1%, leads on local inference speed ~35 tok/s RTX 4090
Llama 4 Scout: 109B total parameters but trails on reasoning benchmarks
RTX 4090 (24GB VRAM): best single-GPU for 7B-30B models, 100-140 tok/s on Llama 3 8B
Apple M4 Max: 85-90% of RTX 4090 performance, superior for 70B+ models via unified memory
70B models require 40-45GB total — impossible on single RTX 4090 without aggressive quantization

stats.fact: 67% of organizations worldwide have adopted LLMs to support their operations with generative AI as of 2025
Local LLM execution has reduced average AI response times from 1.5 seconds to under 40 milliseconds for enterprise tasks
Qwen 3.5 27B achieves ~35 tok/s on RTX 4090 with Q4 quantization — fastest consumer-grade local inference
Qwen 3.5 GPQA Diamond: 88.4%, SWE-bench Verified: 76.4% — surpassing DeepSeek R1 (49.2%)
Gemma 4 31B scores 85 tok/s on consumer hardware, MMLU-Pro 85.2%, AIME 2026 89.2%
RTX 4090 provides 24GB VRAM at 1,008 GB/s bandwidth — optimal single-GPU for 7B-30B local models
Apple M4 Max unified memory achieves >0.5 TB/s throughput — the only single-device consumer option for 70B+ models
stats.source: SitePoint — Mac vs PC Local LLM Hardware 2026

stats.fact: MoE architecture now dominates 2026 flagship open-weight models: DeepSeek V4-Pro (1.6T total/49B active), Qwen 3.5 (397B/17B active), Llama 4 Maverick (400B/17B active)
Phi-4 (14B) scores 80.4% on MATH benchmark, outperforming Llama 3.3 8B (68.0%) despite smaller category
Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B achieves ~85% on HumanEval, vs 68% for Llama 3.3 8B — top coding local model

angles: Hardware-first framing: help readers identify the right model based on their existing GPU/RAM setup
2026 MoE architecture shift — why sparse models change the local LLM calculus (more params, same VRAM)
Apple Silicon vs NVIDIA GPU showdown for local inference — when each wins
The privacy and sovereignty argument: why enterprises are moving inference on-prem
Quantization guide: Q4 vs Q5 vs Q8 — the speed vs quality tradeoff explained for beginners
Use-case specific picks: best local model for coding, writing, reasoning, and RAG
Cost analysis: total cost of ownership for local inference vs cloud API at scale
outline: H2: Why Run an LLM Locally in 2026? (Privacy, Cost, Latency)
H2: The 2026 Local LLM Landscape: MoE Models Change Everything
H2: Hardware Tiers: What You Can Run on Your Setup
H2: Top Local LLM Models of 2026 — Head-to-Head Benchmarks

H2: Ollama vs LM Studio: Which Tool to Use in 2026

H2: Final Verdict: Our Top Picks by Hardware Tier
